title: Slow Clients, Resource Limits, Backpressure, Admission, and Cleanup
description: A server gives every connection a goroutine and unlimited buffer, refreshes deadlines on single-byte trickles, queues output without bounds, and retries accept failures until the process collapses. Learners cumulatively produce connection resource budget and overload policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.
Bound per-connection buffers, retained slices, field maps, content queues, trailer data, and pooled-object capacity before allocation.
Propagate slow peer writes through bounded queues, cancellation, deadlines, partial output evidence, and producer shutdown.
